rationale: The citing paper is a meta-analysis that explicitly compiles econometric brand-level advertising-elasticity studies from 1960–2008 (56 publications), and the cited 2008 paper is exactly an econometric scanner-data study estimating own-brand advertising elasticity (with GRPs/carryover and competitive effects). Given this direct construct and time-window fit, the citation is plausibly a study-level input or substantive evidence source rather than a generic mention.
